channel.title: Rockwell Automation Vulnerability Intelligence
channel.description: Latest Rockwell Automation vulnerability intelligence from CISA, NVD, and security news

item.title: What to Know About CyberAv3ngers: The IRGC-Linked Group Targeting Critical Infrastructure - Security Boulevard
item.description: What to Know About CyberAv3ngers: The IRGC-Linked Group Targeting Critical Infrastructure Security Boulevard

item.title: Iran-linked hackers target water, energy in US, FBI and CISA warn - Cybersecurity Dive

item.title: SD1771 | IMPORTANT NOTICE: Rockwell Automation Reiterates Customer Guidance to Disconnect Devices from the Internet and Harden PLCs to Protect from Cyber Threats
Rockwell Vulnerability Allowing Remote ICS Hacking Exploited in Attacks - SecurityWeek
item.description: Rockwell Vulnerability Allowing Remote ICS Hacking Exploited in Attacks SecurityWeek

item.title: CISA Flags Hikvision Camera & Rockwell Logix Vulnerabilities as Actively Exploited - SOCRadar® Cyber Intelligence Inc.
item.description: CISA Flags Hikvision Camera & Rockwell Logix Vulnerabilities as Actively Exploited SOCRadar® Cyber Intelligence Inc.

item.title: 3 Apple flaws from Coruna exploit kit added to CISA vulnerability list - SC Media
item.description: 3 Apple flaws from Coruna exploit kit added to CISA vulnerability list SC Media

item.title: Hikvision and Rockwell Automation CVSS 9.8 Flaws Added to CISA KEV Catalog - The Hacker News
item.description: Hikvision and Rockwell Automation CVSS 9.8 Flaws Added to CISA KEV Catalog The Hacker News

item.title: CVE-2021-22681 - Multiple Products
item.description: Multiple Rockwell products contain an insufficient protected credentials vulnerability. Studio 5000 Logix Designer software may allow a key to be discovered. This key is used to verify Logix controllers are communicating with Rockwell Automation design software. If successfully exploited, this vulnerability could allow an unauthorized application to connect with Logix controllers. To leverage this vulnerability, an unauthorized user would require network access to the controller.

item.title: Rockwell Automation ControlLogix
item.description: Successful exploitation of this vulnerability could allow an attacker to cause a denial-of-service condition.

item.title: Rockwell Automation ArmorStart LT
item.description: Successful exploitation of these vulnerabilities could allow an attacker to cause a denial-of-service condition.

item.title: Rockwell Automation CompactLogix 5370
item.description: Successful exploitation of this vulnerability could allow an attacker to cause a denial-of-service condition.

item.title: Rockwell Automation Verve Asset Manager
item.description: Successful exploitation of these vulnerabilities may allow an attacker to access sensitive information stored in variables within the ADI server.

item.title: SD1768 | ArmorStart® LT - Multiple Denial-of-Service Vulnerabilities
SD1767 | Verve Asset Manager – Plaintext Storage Vulnerabilities
SD1770 | CompactLogix® 5370 Denial of Service Vulnerability
SD1769 | IMPORTANT NOTICE: Rockwell Automation Recommends Upgrading From 1756-RM2(XT) To 1756-RM3(XT)

item.description: CISA issues multiple ICS advisories, details DoS vulnerability risk in Rockwell devices used in critical manufacturing Industrial Cyber

item.title: Rockwell Automation FactoryTalk DataMosaix Private Cloud
item.description: Successful exploitation of this vulnerability could allow an attacker to perform unauthorized sensitive database operations.

item.title: Rockwell Automation 432ES-IG3 Series A
item.description: Successful exploitation of this vulnerability could result in a denial-of-service condition.

item.title: Rockwell Automation Micro820, Micro850, Micro870
item.description: Successful exploitation of these vulnerabilities could result in a denial-of-service condition.

item.title: SD1766 | Micro820®, Micro850®, Micro870® – Specialized Fuzzing Vulnerabilities
SD1765 | FactoryTalk® DataMosaix™ Private Cloud SQL Injection
SD1764 | 432ES-IG3 Series A Denial-of-Service Vulnerability

item.description: Weekly IT & ICS Vulnerabilities Report — Top Risks of 2025 Cyble

item.title: Rockwell Automation Arena Simulation
item.description: Successful exploitation of this vulnerability could allow local attackers to execute arbitrary code on affected installations of Arena.

item.title: SD1763 | Arena® Simulation Stack-Based Buffer Overflow Vulnerability
*Rockwell Automation AADvance-Trusted SIS Workstation *
item.description: Successful exploitation of this vulnerability may allow remote code execution.

item.title: Rockwell Automation FactoryTalk Policy Manager
item.description: Successful exploitation of this vulnerability could lead to resource exhaustion and denial of service.

item.title: Rockwell Automation FactoryTalk DataMosaix Private Cloud
item.description: Successful exploitation of these vulnerabilities could allow an attacker to take over accounts, steal credentials, redirect users to a malicious website, or bypass MFA.

item.title: Rockwell Automation Studio 5000 Simulation Interface
item.description: Successful exploitation of these vulnerabilities could allow attackers to trigger outbound SMB requests to capture NTLM hashes and execute scripts with Administrator privileges upon system reboot.

item.title: Rockwell Automation Verve Asset Manager
item.description: Successful exploitation of this vulnerability could result in an attacker accessing or altering user data.

item.title: SD1760 | Studio 5000 ® Simulation Interface - Multiple Vulnerabilities
SD1759 | Verve Asset Manager Access Control Vulnerability
SD1758 | FactoryTalk® DataMosaix™ Private Cloud – Multiple Vulnerabilities
SD1761 | SIS Workstation DotNetZip Third-Party Vulnerability CVE-2024-48510
SD1762 | FactoryTalk® Policy Manager Third-Party Vulnerability
Rockwell Automation Compact GuardLogix 5370
item.description: Successful exploitation of this vulnerability could result in a denial-of-service.

item.title: Rockwell Automation 1783-NATR
item.description: Successful exploitation of these vulnerabilities could result in a denial-of-service, data modification, or in an attacker obtaining sensitive information.

item.title: Rockwell Automation ArmorStart AOP
item.description: Successful exploitation of this vulnerability could allow an attacker to cause a denial-of-service condition on the affected product.

item.title: Rockwell Automation FactoryTalk ViewPoint
item.description: Successful exploitation of this vulnerability could allow unauthenticated attackers to achieve XML external entity injection, resulting in a temporary denial-of-service condition.

item.title: Rockwell Automation FactoryTalk Linx
item.description: Successful exploitation of these vulnerabilities may allow full access to all files, processes, and system resources.

item.title: Rockwell Automation FactoryTalk View Machine Edition and PanelView Plus 7
item.description: Successful exploitation of these vulnerabilities could allow an unauthenticated attacker to access to the device's file system.

item.title: Rockwell Automation 1715 EtherNet/IP Comms Module
item.description: Successful exploitation of these vulnerabilities could allow an attacker to cause the web server to crash, requiring a restart to recover.

item.title: SD1754 | FactoryTalk® Linx Privilege Escalation Vulnerabilities
SD1756 | Comms - 1783-NATR Multiple Vulnerabilities
SD1753 | FactoryTalk View Machine Edition and PanelView Plus 7 Vulnerabilities
SD1755 | Compact GuardLogix® 5370 Denial-Of-Service Vulnerability
